title: Coverage eval — determine advisor needs from goals + recent concerns
perceive: Re-determine what advisors this spoke has versus what its current goals and recent concerns require. Follow Ozi's Team Coverage Evaluation in WAI-Harness/spoke/advisors/ozi/context_prompt.md. Roster: WAI-Harness/spoke/advisors/registry.json. Hub scope patterns: /home/mario/projects/wheelwright/hub/WAI-Hub/advisors/octo/advisor-recommendation-patterns.json.
execute: 2. Read WAI-Harness/spoke/local/WAI-State.json for project goals/identity and scan the last 30 open lugs' titles for recent concerns/keywords.
3. Read /home/mario/projects/wheelwright/hub/WAI-Hub/advisors/octo/advisor-recommendation-patterns.json; match active scopes against each scope_trigger's detection_signals.

4. For each matched scope_trigger whose advisor_ids_to_check are absent from the roster, record one gap object using the field name 'domain' (not 'scope') set to the scope_trigger string. Example gap: {"domain": "deployment_automation", "suggested_template": "engineering-advisor", "priority": 3, "justification": "one line reason", "proposal_status": "pending"}.

5. Read WAI-Harness/spoke/advisors/ozi/scan_state.json (create {} if absent). Write back the ENTIRE file with a top-level 'team_coverage' key — do NOT write last_coverage_eval_at, active_scopes, or gaps_detected at the root. Required structure: {"team_coverage": {"last_coverage_eval_at": "<now ISO8601>", "active_scopes": [<scope_trigger strings>], "gaps_detected": [<gap objects from step 4>]}, <...preserve other existing top-level keys...>}.
6. Do NOT provision advisors here. Recommendations become user-review lugs on the next scouting pass.
verify: team_coverage.last_coverage_eval_at updated to today; gaps_detected[] reflects the current roster-vs-goals analysis with a justification per gap; no advisor directories were created.
